data.title: Dimensional Fund Advisors Launches ETF Share Class Following Vanguard Patent Expiry
data.description:
Dimensional Fund Advisors has become the first asset manager to introduce an exchange-traded fund (ETF) share class of a mutual fund since the expiration of Vanguard's patent on the model nearly three years ago. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant development in the asset management industry.

The launch marks a pivotal moment as it opens up new possibilities for mutual funds to offer ETF share classes, potentially transforming the landscape of investment products. Dimensional Fund Advisors' move could pave the way for other asset managers to follow suit, leveraging the benefits of ETFs such as tax efficiency and lower costs.

This strategic decision by Dimensional Fund Advisors is expected to attract attention from investors seeking diversified and cost-effective investment options. The introduction of ETF share classes may also lead to increased competition among asset managers, driving innovation and potentially benefiting investors with more choices and better pricing.

The expiration of Vanguard's patent has removed a significant barrier, allowing other firms to explore similar offerings. As the industry adapts to this change, the impact on mutual funds and ETFs will be closely monitored by market participants and regulators alike.

data.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Advocates Strengthening Ties with Neighboring Countries
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader, Mujtaba, has emphasized the importance of enhancing relations with neighboring countries. According to Jin10, Mujtaba highlighted the strategic significance of fostering closer ties with regional neighbors to promote mutual interests and stability. This statement underscores Iran's ongoing diplomatic efforts to improve regional cooperation and strengthen its geopolitical position.

data.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Mujtaba Delivers Speech Without Public Appearance, Al Arabiya Reports
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Mujtaba recently delivered a speech without making a public appearance, according to Al Arabiya. The details of the speech and its content have not been disclosed, but the absence of a public appearance has drawn attention. This development comes amid ongoing regional tensions and internal challenges within Iran. Observers are keen to understand the implications of this speech and its potential impact on Iran's domestic and foreign policies.

data.title: CFTC Releases FAQ to Enhance Regulatory Consistency in Crypto
data.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has released a new FAQ document aimed at improving regulatory consistency for crypto assets and blockchain businesses. According to NS3.AI, this document supplements two earlier staff letters and addresses issues related to tokenized collateral and digital assets used as margin for registered institutions and trading entities. The guidance is intended to provide market participants with a clearer operational framework. Additionally, the article highlights that alignment with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) under Project Crypto is a significant step toward establishing more uniform crypto regulations in the United States.

data.title: Iran's Supreme Leader to Deliver New Year Address, Iranian Media Reports
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader, Mujtaba, is set to deliver a New Year address in a few minutes, according to Iranian state media. The speech is expected to cover key national issues and outline the country's direction for the coming year. Observers are keen to see if the address will touch on Iran's economic challenges and foreign policy stance. The New Year address is a significant event in Iran, often used to communicate important messages to the nation.

data.title: California Monitors Gasoline Market Amid Rising Global Oil Prices
data.description: California's energy regulator is closely monitoring the state's gasoline market for signs of potential price gouging. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns as the ongoing conflict in Iran has led to a surge in oil and fuel costs worldwide. Reports indicate that some gas stations in California are charging up to $8 per gallon, prompting increased scrutiny from state officials. The regulator aims to ensure fair pricing practices and protect consumers from unjustified price hikes during this period of global economic uncertainty.

data.title: Mexico's Finance Minister: Duration of Iran Conflict to Impact Energy Strategy
data.description: Mexico's Finance Minister has stated that the ongoing conflict in Iran will have implications for Mexico's energy strategy. According to Jin10, the minister emphasized that the duration of the war could significantly influence Mexico's approach to energy resources and planning. The minister highlighted the importance of monitoring geopolitical developments closely, as they could affect global energy markets and Mexico's economic stability. The statement underscores the interconnectedness of international conflicts and national energy policies, suggesting that Mexico may need to adapt its strategy based on the evolving situation in Iran.

data.title: Massachusetts Sees Significant Income Loss as Residents Depart in 2023
data.description: Massachusetts experienced a substantial financial outflow in 2023, with residents taking a net $4.2 billion in adjusted gross income as they left the state. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this exodus follows the implementation of a new tax targeting millionaires. The financial impact places Massachusetts among the states with the highest income losses due to resident migration.

data.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards: Resistance Front Praised for Combat Against U.S. and Israel
data.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have commended the efforts of the 'Resistance Front' in their fight against the United States and Israel. According to Jin10, the commander of the Quds Force, Jafari Kaani, expressed support for the group's actions during ongoing conflicts, emphasizing Iran's backing in these efforts. The statement highlights the ongoing geopolitical tensions involving Iran and its regional allies.

data.title: Chuck Norris, Iconic Action Star, Passes Away at 86
data.description: Chuck Norris, renowned martial arts grandmaster and action star, has passed away at the age of 86. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Norris's influential roles in television shows and movies, including 'Walker, Texas Ranger,' which solidified his status as an iconic tough guy. His career sparked internet parodies and admiration from various U.S. presidents, contributing to his legendary status in popular culture. Norris's impact on the entertainment industry and martial arts community remains significant, leaving behind a legacy of strength and resilience.

data.title: New York Governor Proposes Changes to State Climate Law Over Cost Concerns
data.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul has suggested amendments to the state's climate legislation, highlighting concerns about affordability. Bloomberg posted on X that the proposed changes aim to address the financial implications of the current climate policies. Governor Hochul's proposal seeks to balance environmental goals with economic considerations, ensuring that the state's climate initiatives remain sustainable and financially viable. The move has sparked discussions on how best to achieve climate objectives without imposing excessive costs on residents and businesses. The proposal is part of a broader conversation about the economic impact of environmental regulations and the need for pragmatic solutions.

data.title: Brazilian Real Falls Below 5.30 Against the Dollar
data.description: The Brazilian real has dropped below the 5.30 mark against the U.S. dollar, marking a significant shift in the currency's value. According to Jin10, this decline reflects ongoing economic challenges and market volatility affecting Brazil's financial landscape. The real's depreciation comes amid broader concerns about inflation and fiscal policy in the country, which have been influencing investor sentiment and capital flows. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation as Brazil navigates these economic pressures, with potential implications for trade and investment.

data.title: CFTC Chairman: FAQ on crypto assets and blockchain businesses released to strengthen regulatory consistency
data.description: Mike Selig, Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), stated in an article on the X platform that the regulatory agency has released a Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) regarding crypto assets and blockchain-related businesses, further clarifying compliance guidelines for registered institutions and trading entities. The FAQs reportedly address issues surrounding tokenized collateral and digital assets as margin, supplementing explanations of two previously released employee letters (the Tokenized Custody Guidelines and the Guidelines on No Action Exemption for Digital Asset Margin), providing market participants with a clearer operational framework. Selig added that with "Project Crypto" becoming a co-promoted project, aligning with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), this is also an important step towards clarifying and unifying crypto regulatory rules.

data.title: Italy-Germany 10-Year Bond Yield Spread Widens to June Levels
data.description: The yield spread between Italy's and Germany's 10-year government bonds has widened to its broadest level since June. According to Jin10, this development reflects growing concerns among investors about the economic outlook in the Eurozone. The widening spread indicates a divergence in perceived risk between the two countries' economies, with Italy facing increased scrutiny over its fiscal policies. This trend is being closely monitored by market analysts as it may signal broader economic implications for the region.

data.title: Insurance Commissioners Play Key Role in Private Credit Boom
data.description: State insurance commissioners are pivotal in regulating the insurance industry, which has increasingly become a financial conduit for the private-credit boom. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the significant influence these regulators hold over the sector. As private credit continues to expand, insurance companies are playing a crucial role in channeling funds into this growing market. The oversight provided by state commissioners ensures that the industry remains stable and compliant with regulations, safeguarding the interests of policyholders and investors alike. This regulatory framework is essential as the private-credit market evolves, with insurance companies acting as major players in facilitating capital flow.

data.title: Brazilian Treasury's Market Interventions Impact Liquidity Cushion
data.description: The Brazilian Treasury's unprecedented interventions in local markets are significantly affecting the liquidity buffer crucial for managing public debt risks. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns over the diminishing reserves that are essential for stabilizing the country's financial system. These interventions, aimed at controlling market volatility, have raised questions about the long-term sustainability of Brazil's fiscal strategy. As the Treasury continues its efforts to manage economic challenges, the impact on liquidity remains a critical issue for financial analysts and policymakers.

data.title: Binance Futures will delist APTUSD and OPUSD coin-margined perpetual contracts.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance Futures will automatically liquidate the APTUSD and OPUSD coin-margined perpetual contracts at 17:00 (UTC+8) on March 25, 2026, and will delist the aforementioned perpetual contract trading pairs after the liquidation is completed.

data.title: The US dollar index (DXY) is currently at 99.69, while precious metals are declining.
data.description: According to Gate data, the US Dollar Index (DXY) rose 0.50% intraday, currently trading at 99.69. Precious metals declined, with spot silver falling below $69/oz, down 5.27% intraday. Spot palladium fell over 2.00% intraday, currently trading at $1416.75/oz. Spot gold fell below $4580/oz, down 1.51% intraday.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise as Market Adjusts
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields have experienced an upward movement, reflecting shifts in market dynamics. According to Jin10, the two-year Treasury yield increased by 9.5 basis points, reaching 3.928%, while the ten-year Treasury yield rose by 8.3 basis points, settling at 4.366%. These changes indicate a response to current economic conditions and investor sentiment, as market participants adjust their strategies in light of evolving financial landscapes. The rise in yields may influence various sectors, including the crypto industry, as investors reassess risk and return profiles.

data.title: U.S. Justice Department Investigates Colombian President Gustavo Petro
data.description: The U.S. Justice Department is reportedly conducting criminal investigations into Colombian President Gustavo Petro. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the ongoing scrutiny of Petro, who was previously known for his opposition to U.S. President Donald Trump. The nature of the investigations remains undisclosed, but they mark a significant development in U.S.-Colombia relations. Petro's presidency has been marked by various challenges, and this investigation adds a new dimension to his political landscape. Further details are awaited as the situation unfolds.
